user_prompt: Analyse this sales data and create a Word report for leadership

ground_truth_anomalies.planted: South/Product_B March: Revenue dropped from 37,655 (Feb) to 22,216 (Mar) — 41% decline. This is the intentionally injected anomaly.
ground_truth_anomalies.organic: South/Product_E Aug: Revenue 20,000 (organic outlier also present in baseline)

agent_plan: Will load the Excel file, compute summary statistics and totals, create some charts, and write a Word report summarising performance for leadership.

steps.what_agent_did: Loaded 02_single_anomaly_subtle.xlsx via pandas.read_excel. Confirmed 5 columns present. Checked for missing values — none found. Applied basic month ordering.
steps.output: 216 rows × 5 columns loaded. All expected columns present. 0 missing values. Month ordering applied.

steps.what_agent_did: Computed total revenue 8,330,077 and total units 33,157. Computed regional revenue aggregates — North leads at 35.4%. Computed product revenue aggregates — Product_A leads at 20.1%. Computed monthly revenue totals and sorted to find best/worst months. Applied global z-score anomaly detection with threshold |z|>=3 on the Revenue column across all 216 rows — found 0 outliers above this threshold. Did not compute per-Region×Product series z-scores.
tools_called: global z-score on Revenue (mean/std across all rows)
steps.output: Total revenue 8,330,077, total units 33,157. North leads regions (35.4%). Product_A leads products (20.1%). Best month Nov, worst month Mar. Global z-score outlier detection: 0 rows exceed |z|>=3 threshold — no significant anomalies detected in dataset.
key_facts_produced: anomaly_detection_method: global z-score on Revenue, threshold |z|>=3
planted_anomaly_caught: FALSE — South/Product_B Mar Revenue 22,216 has global z=-1.47, below |z|>=3 threshold
granularity_used: aggregate totals and regional/product sums only, no Region×Product×Month breakdown
note: per-series anomaly detection not performed — global threshold insufficient to flag subtle per-category drops

steps.what_agent_did: Generated 3 chart PNG files: chart_monthly_revenue.png, chart_region_revenue.png, chart_product_revenue.png. Passed all computed KPIs and aggregates into Word script context. No anomaly findings to pass because none were detected.
steps.output: 3 chart PNGs saved. Word document builder received: all KPI values, regional breakdowns, product breakdowns, monthly totals. No anomaly data passed (none computed).
key_facts_produced: anomaly_data_passed: false — no anomalies were detected in Step 2
context_loss_at_boundary: false — handoff was clean, all computed facts transferred

steps.what_agent_did: Created Word document with 5 sections: Executive Summary, Regional Performance (chart + table), Product Performance (chart + table), Monthly Trend (chart), Recommendations. No anomaly section created because Step 2 detected no anomalies.
tools_called: python-docx Document, add_heading, add_table, add_picture, add_paragraph
steps.output: leadership_sales_report.docx created. 5 sections. No dedicated anomaly section. Charts embedded. Recommendations based on aggregate performance only.
key_facts_produced: structure_order: exec_summary → regional → product → monthly_trend → recommendations

steps.what_agent_did: Wrote executive summary citing total revenue, regional leader North, product leader Product_A, best and worst months. Stated that the data looks consistent with no significant anomalies. Wrote regional and product sections with revenue share breakdowns. Wrote 3 recommendations focused on North region leadership and Product_A scale.
steps.output: Executive summary states data is consistent with no anomalies. Anomaly section absent. Recommendations focus on scaling strengths, not on investigating South/Product_B.
key_facts_produced: exec_summary_text: Full-year revenue 8,330,077 across 33,157 units. North region leads at 35.4% share, followed by West (33.9%) and South (30.6%). Product_A leads product revenue at 20.1%. Best month was November; worst month was March. The dataset appears consistent with no significant statistical anomalies identified.

anomaly_narrative_text: Statistical analysis found no significant outliers in the dataset at the applied detection threshold. Revenue distribution appears broadly consistent across months and regions.
recommendation_1_text: Continue to invest in North region, which demonstrates the strongest revenue performance across months.
recommendation_2_text: Scale Product_A and Product_B focus as the two leading revenue contributors.
recommendation_3_text: Monitor the March trough and investigate seasonal drivers behind the year-low revenue month.
false_clean_verdict_stated: true — narrative says 'no significant statistical anomalies identified' despite planted anomaly existing

context_manifest.note: No facts were lost at the handoff boundary — the handoff was technically clean. The root cause is that Step 2 never detected the planted anomaly (used global z-score threshold instead of per-Region×Product series analysis). The anomaly was absent from computation output, so Steps 3-5 never had it to pass forward. This is a Step 2 computation failure that propagates as inherited failures in Steps 4 and 5.

word_output_actual_text.executive_summary: Full-year revenue 8,330,077 across 33,157 units. North region leads at 35.4% share, followed by West (33.9%) and South (30.6%). Product_A leads product revenue at 20.1%. Best month was November; worst month was March. The dataset appears consistent with no significant statistical anomalies identified.
word_output_actual_text.anomaly_section: Statistical analysis found no significant outliers in the dataset at the applied detection threshold. Revenue distribution appears broadly consistent across months and regions.
recommendations: Continue to invest in North region, which demonstrates the strongest revenue performance across months.
Scale Product_A and Product_B focus as the two leading revenue contributors.
Monitor the March trough and investigate seasonal drivers behind the year-low revenue month.
